ace: noun. A playing card showing a single pip, typically the highest or lowest ranking card in a game.
acre: noun. An English unit of land area (symbol: a. or ac.) originally denoting a day 's ploughing for a yoke of oxen, now standardized as 4,840
age: noun. The amount of time that some being has been alive, or that some thing has been in existence, as measured from its birth or origin
ager: noun. One who or that which ages something.
agile: adjective. Having the faculty of quick motion in the limbs; apt or ready to move; loose-jointed.
ail: verb. To cause to suffer; to trouble, afflict. (Now chiefly in interrogative or indefinite constructions.)
air: noun. The substance constituting Earth 's atmosphere: a gaseous mixture of nitrogen, oxygen, and various trace gases.
ale: noun. A beer made without hops.
alec: noun. An anchovy or herring, especially pickled or dried.
all: determiner. Every individual or anything of the given class, with no exceptions (the noun or noun phrase denoting the class must be plural
allergic: adjective. Of or pertaining to allergy.
arc: noun. That part of a circle which a heavenly body appears to pass through as it moves above and below the horizon.
are: verb. second-person singular simple present of be
areic: adjective. Of or pertaining to area; especially used to describe a measurement per unit area.
ariel: noun. A kind of mountain gazelle, native to Arabia.
aril: noun. A tissue surrounding the seed in certain fruits such as pomegranates.
cage: noun. An enclosure made of spaced bars, normally to hold animals while providing some ventilation.
cager: noun. A person or machine responsible for managing a mineshaft cage.
cagier: adjective. comparative form of cagey: more cagey
call: verb. To reach out with one's voice.
caller: noun. The person who makes a telephone call.
car: noun. A wheeled vehicle that moves independently, with at least three wheels, powered mechanically, steered by a driver and mostly for
carl: noun. A rude, rustic man; a churl.
ceil: verb. To line or finish (a surface, such as a wall), with plaster, stucco, thin boards, or similar.
cel: noun. A piece of celluloid on which has been drawn a frame of an animated film.
cell: noun. A single - room dwelling for a hermit.
cella: noun. The central, enclosed part of an ancient temple, as distinguished from the open porticos.
cellar: noun. An enclosed underground space, often under a building, used for storage or shelter.
cigar: noun. A cylinder of tobacco rolled and wrapped with an outer covering of tobacco leaves, intended to be smoked.
cire: noun. A fabric with a glazed finish.
clag: noun. A glue or paste made from starch.
crag: noun. A rocky outcrop; a rugged steep cliff or rock.
ear: noun. The organ of hearing, consisting of the pinna or auricle, auditory canal, eardrum, malleus, incus, stapes and cochlea.
earl: noun. A British or Irish nobleman next in rank above a viscount and below a marquess; equivalent to a European count.
era: noun. A time period of indeterminate length, generally more than one year.
erg: noun. A unit of work or energy, being the amount of work done by a force of one dyne applied through a distance of one centimeter.
erica: noun. Any of many heathers, of the genus Erica, used as garden plants
gal: noun. A young woman or girl; generally a form of rural Southern slang.
gall: noun. Impudence or brazenness; temerity; chutzpah.
gallic: adjective. Of, pertaining to, or derived from galls.
gar: noun. Any of several North American fish of the family Lepisosteidae that have long, narrow jaws.

gear: noun. Equipment or paraphernalia, especially that used for an athletic endeavor.
gel: noun. A semi-solid to almost solid colloid of a solid and a liquid, such as jelly, cheese or opal.
gie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Guébie.
gill: noun. A breathing organ of fish and other aquatic animals.
giller: noun. A person who gills fish.
glacier: noun. A large body of ice which flows under its own mass, usually downhill.
glair: noun. Egg white, especially as used in various industrial preparations.
glia: noun. The network of glial cells that supports nervous system tissue.
glial: adjective. Of or pertaining to glia.
grill: noun. A grating; a grid of wire or a sheet of material with a pattern of holes or slots, usually used to protect something while allowing
grille: noun. Alternative form of grill (only in the senses of "grating over opening", "grating on the front of a vehicle", and "window divider")
ileal: adjective. Of or pertaining to the ileum.
iller: adjective. comparative form of ill: more ill
lac: noun. A resinous substance or lacquer produced mainly on the banyan tree by the female of Kerria lacca, a scale insect.
lace: noun. A light fabric containing patterns of holes, usually built up from a single thread.
lacer: noun. A person or thing that laces.
lacier: adjective. comparative form of lacy: more lacy
lager: noun. A type of beer, brewed using a bottom-fermenting yeast.
laic: noun. A layperson as opposed to a cleric, i.e. not a member of the clergy.
lair: noun. A place inhabited by a wild animal, often a cave or a hole in the ground.
lar: noun. singular of lares: a household god, particularly overseeing the family itself.
large: adjective. Of considerable or relatively great size or extent.
lari: noun. The national currency of Georgia, divided into 100 tetri.
lea: noun. An open field, meadow, pasture.
leg: noun. A limb or appendage that an animal uses for support or locomotion on land.
legal: adjective. Relating to the law or to lawyers.
lei: noun. A garland of flowers in Hawaii.
liar: noun. Someone who tells a lie; especially, a person who frequently lies.
lie: verb. To rest in a horizontal position on a surface.
lier: noun. A person or thing that lies, in the sense of being horizontal.
liger: noun. An animal born to a male lion and a tigress.
lilac: noun. A large shrub of the genus Syringa, especially Syringa vulgaris, bearing white, pale- pink, or purple flowers.
lira: noun. The basic unit of currency in Turkey.
lire: noun. Flesh, brawn, or muscle; the fleshy part of a person or animal in contradistinction to the bone and skin.
race: noun. A contest between people, animals, vehicles, etc. where the goal is to be the first to reach some objective.
ragi: noun. A grain, of species Eleusine coracana, cultivated as a cereal in arid areas of Africa and Asia; finger millet.
rail: noun. A horizontal bar extending between supports and used for support or as a barrier; a railing.
rale: noun. An abnormal clicking, rattling or crackling sound, made by one or both lungs and heard with a stethoscope, caused by the popping open
real: adjective. True, genuine, not merely nominal or apparent.
recall: verb. To withdraw, retract (one's words etc.); to revoke (an order).
regal: adjective. Of, pertaining to, or suitable for royalty.
relic: noun. That which remains; that which is left after loss or decay; a remaining portion.
ria: noun. A narrow inlet of the sea formed by a flooded unglaciated river valley.
rial: noun. The official currencies of Iran, Oman, and Yemen.
rice: noun. Cereal plants, Oryza sativa of the grass family whose seeds are used as food.
riel: noun. The monetary unit of Cambodia, equivalent to 100 sen or 10 kak.
rig: noun. The rigging of a sailing ship or other such craft.
rile: verb. To stir or move from a state of calm or order.
rill: noun. A very small brook; a streamlet; a creek, rivulet.
rille: noun. A long, narrow depression that resembles a channel, found on the surface of various lunar and planetary bodies.
acer: noun. A plant of the genus Acer; a maple.
acle: noun. A kind of ironwood tree (Xylia xylocarpa).
agiler: adjective. comparative form of agile: more agile
aiger: noun. Alternative form of eagre; a tidal bore.
allice: noun. A fish, the allis shad (Alosa alosa).
arg: noun. An argument; a value passed as a parameter.
cill: noun. Alternative form of sill (“a threshold or brink across the bottom of a canal lock for the gates to shut against”).
claire: noun. A small enclosed pond used for gathering and greening oysters.
cleg: noun. a blood-sucking fly of the family Tabanidae: gadfly, horsefly, deer fly, blind-fly, tabanid
crile: noun. A small person or creature; a dwarf or runt.
crl: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Northern East Cree.
eir: determiner. Belonging to em, gender-neutral third-person singular possessive adjective, equivalent to the singular their and coordinate
eli: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nding.

grallic: adjective. Of or relating to the Grallae, a former taxonomic classification of wading birds.

lig: verb. To lie; be in a prostrate or recumbent position.
